danger_signals.visual_form: tiles filling up the well columns toward the top
danger_signals.meaning: player is losing; if well fills completely, game ends
cannot catch additional tiles without penalty; risk of tiles falling off conveyor
uncaught tiles or missed catches; running out of allowed drops ends the game

safety_signals.visual_form: three or more matching colored tiles in horizontal/vertical/diagonal lines disappear with visual feedback
safety_signals.meaning: player is succeeding; tiles clear and well space opens up

safety_signals.visual_form: gaps and low tile density in the five-column well
safety_signals.meaning: player has room to maneuver and catch more tiles safely

safety_signals.visual_form: multiple Klaxs formed in cascade from single tile drop
safety_signals.meaning: optimal play state; cascading matches award increasing points

faction_indicators.color_identity: multi-color tile system (player-interacts with 'various colors' of tiles)

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: slower tile descent speed down conveyor belt; fewer tiles flowing
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player has time to position paddle and make deliberate matches

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: faster tile descent; higher frequency of colored tiles arriving
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player must react quickly; drop penalty risk increases; well fills faster

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: menu presenting three wave options with difficulty/bonus indicators
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: player chooses next wave; later waves = higher bonus points but higher risk

world_state_to_visual.visual_manifestation: well completely filled with tiles OR drop meter exhausted
world_state_to_visual.behavioral_manifestation: play ceases; game ends; final score calculated

environmental_storytelling.example: tiles physically roll down the belt toward paddle; player learns through spatial interaction that catching and positioning matters
100 waves organized in 20 groups of 5 creates sense of escalating challenge; drop meter resets every 5 waves, signaling checkpoint/rest state
colored tiles create visual pattern language; matching three same colors is core objective, teaching pattern recognition as core mechanic
five-column well with five-tile-height limit visually communicates resource scarcity and spatial puzzle solving

entity_behavioral_tells.behavior: advances downward at speed determined by wave difficulty; player can flip tile up the belt using up joystick
moves left/right along conveyor; holds up to five tiles; pressing button drops topmost tile into well directly below
any tiles above a cleared Klax pattern fall downward to fill gaps; gravity-based cascade creates potential for chained matches
presents player with three wave options; choosing later wave increments bonus points available and increases drop limit tolerance

notes: Klax is a pure puzzle game with no faction, political, or narrative world-state systems. Visual communication is entirely mechanical and rule-based. Danger/safety signals are player-state indicators (well fullness, drop count, paddle capacity) rather than environmental or narrative markers. The game communicates challenge through speed variation and tile frequency, not through visual storytelling or world lore. Color is used for matching mechanics, not faction identification.
